goals: Provide a standalone `comfy_diffusion/mask.py` module with mask creation and manipulation functions
Support loading masks from image files with explicit channel selection (alpha, red, green, blue)
Enable conversion between image tensors and mask tensors
Provide mask geometry operations (grow, feather) needed for inpainting workflows
Follow existing project patterns: lazy imports, external libraries (PIL, torch) over ComfyUI node wrappers where possible

userStories.title: Load mask from image file
userStories.description: As a Python developer using comfy-diffusion, I want to load a mask from an image file choosing which channel to use (alpha, red, green, blue) so that I can obtain mask tensors from standard image files for inpainting or compositing.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `load_image_mask(path, channel)` accepts `str | Path` and a channel parameter
Supported channels: `"alpha"`, `"red"`, `"green"`, `"blue"`
Returns a float32 mask tensor with shape `(1, H, W)` and values in `[0.0, 1.0]`
For alpha channel: transparent = 1.0 (masked), opaque = 0.0 (unmasked), matching ComfyUI convention
For color channels: pixel value 255 → 1.0, pixel value 0 → 0.0
Raises `FileNotFoundError` if the image path does not exist
Typecheck / lint passes

userStories.title: Convert image tensor to mask tensor
userStories.description: As a Python developer using comfy-diffusion, I want to convert a BHWC image tensor to a mask tensor by selecting a channel so that I can derive masks from existing image data without saving/loading files.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `image_to_mask(image, channel)` accepts a BHWC float32 image tensor and channel name
Supported channels: `"red"`, `"green"`, `"blue"`
Returns a float32 mask tensor with shape matching the batch and spatial dims
Typecheck / lint passes

userStories.title: Convert mask tensor to image tensor
userStories.description: As a Python developer using comfy-diffusion, I want to convert a mask tensor back to a PIL Image so that I can visualize or save masks for debugging and inspection.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `mask_to_image(mask)` accepts a float32 mask tensor
Returns a BHWC float32 image tensor with the mask value replicated across RGB channels
Typecheck / lint passes

userStories.title: Grow mask
userStories.description: As a Python developer using comfy-diffusion, I want to expand or contract a mask by N pixels so that I can adjust mask boundaries for inpainting overlap or tighter crops.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `grow_mask(mask, expand, tapered_corners)` grows the mask by `expand` pixels
Positive `expand` grows (dilates) the mask; negative `expand` shrinks (erodes) it
`tapered_corners` parameter controls whether corners are rounded (True) or square (False)
Works on CPU without GPU
Typecheck / lint passes

userStories.title: Feather mask
userStories.description: As a Python developer using comfy-diffusion, I want to blur the edges of a mask so that I get smooth transitions for compositing and inpainting.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `feather_mask(mask, left, top, right, bottom)` feathers mask edges by the specified pixel amounts per side
Supports independent feathering amounts for each edge
Output values remain in `[0.0, 1.0]`
Works on CPU without GPU
Typecheck / lint passes

functionalRequirements.description: Create `comfy_diffusion/mask.py` module with all five public functions
Use lazy imports for `torch` and `PIL` (no top-level imports of heavy dependencies)
Use external libraries (PIL, torch) for image I/O and tensor ops; only wrap ComfyUI nodes when they provide non-trivial logic not easily replicated
Path parameters use `str | Path` type annotation, consistent with the rest of the codebase
All functions must work on CPU-only environments
Follow the alpha convention from ComfyUI: for alpha masks, transparent = 1.0 (masked), opaque = 0.0
`grow_mask` wraps ComfyUI's `GrowMask` node (non-trivial scipy morphological ops with custom kernels)
`feather_mask` is implemented directly with torch ops (simple per-side linear gradient multiplication)
